title: Claude Code Stop-hook continuation is one-shot; the harness's auto-continue design is a direct Cursor port and is structurally incompatible with Claude Code's Stop hook protocol
expected_behavior: On Claude Code, an autonomous multi-turn /invoke-development-harness run (capped by loop_budget in state.json) should proceed without per-turn manual intervention, matching Cursor's behavior. The harness's own authority chain (budget, blockers, open_questions, selector-vs-checkpoint agreement) should be the only cap on continuation.
observed_behavior: /invoke-development-harness auto-continuation on Claude Code fires exactly ONCE, then stops. The user has to re-type /invoke-development-harness every 1-2 units instead of letting the harness drive the whole sequence. The stop-hook's own 'if input_data.get("stop_hook_active", False): _stop(cwd)' branch is what exits 0 on the second firing. If that branch is removed, Claude Code's built-in loop guard catches the repeated force-continue pattern and stops anyway. There is no official opt-out: stop_hook_active is a session-wide persistent flag, not a per-turn counter.
reproduction_steps: Run /create-development-harness on Claude Code; confirm .claude/settings.local.json wires continue-loop.py as a Stop hook with exit-2 block-continue semantics.
Set .harness/state.json execution.loop_budget to >= 3 and execution.session_count to 0 to give the loop headroom.

Run /invoke-development-harness; complete unit 1. Observe: Claude Code injects the '[Harness iteration N/M] Continue with unit ...' system-reminder and the agent begins unit 2 autonomously. This is the first and only hook-driven continuation.
Complete unit 2. Observe: the agent's turn ends and no system-reminder is injected. Claude Code has stopped. Verify .harness/.invoke-active is deleted (the hook's _stop() path removed it).

Run .harness/scripts/select_next_unit.py manually; confirm it still returns 'found: true' for unit 3 with phase_complete: false. The harness itself is ready to continue -- the stop is coming from Claude Code's hook protocol, not the harness.
Read the current stop_hook_active handling in .claude/hooks/continue-loop.py lines 34-36: this is the exact branch that fires on the second call and ends the loop.
deployment_impact: Not deploy-affecting (skill distribution repo; no runtime). Impacts every Claude Code user of the development-harness skill: every multi-unit run requires manual re-invocation per unit, which materially slows the dogfood loop that is the entire point of agent-forge.
regression_coverage: The failure mode is architectural (host hook-protocol semantics), not a code path with a clean unit-test boundary. The viable regression guards are: (1) a docs-linter test asserting that the Claude Code branch of create.md Phase 5 does NOT install continue-loop.py as a block-continue driver (it installs it as a precondition checker only); (2) a docs-linter test asserting that invoke.md and the workspace-commands template carry a 'Claude Code: run under /loop' section pointing users at '/loop /invoke-development-harness' as the autonomous-run entry point; (3) a unit test of the hook's new precondition-only mode that confirms exit codes: flag-present + preconditions-met -> exit 0 with a stdout advisory, flag-present + preconditions-failed -> exit 0 with a blocker advisory, flag-absent -> exit 0 silently (no-op).
root_cause: skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py (and the provisioned copy at .claude/hooks/continue-loop.py) is a line-for-line port of the Cursor variant at skills/development-harness/templates/hooks/continue-loop.py. The Cursor protocol uses {"followup_message": "..."} with no built-in loop guard -- the harness's loop_budget in state.json is the only cap. Claude Code's Stop hook protocol does the opposite: it exposes a persistent stop_hook_active flag that flips to True after the first hook-driven continuation and stays True for the rest of the session, and it enforces its own loop-guard floor that overrides the hook if the check is bypassed. The skill was built Cursor-first and the port assumed the two protocols were interchangeable. They aren't.
fix_approach: Retire the Stop-hook-as-continuation-driver for the Claude Code variant and move multi-turn autonomy to the native /loop skill (a March 2026 Claude Code primitive). Keep continue-loop.py's authority chain (budget, blockers, open_questions, selector-vs-checkpoint agreement) as a precondition checker, but do not attempt exit-2 block-continue. Concrete skill-source changes: (1) new doc skills/development-harness/references/claude-code-continuation.md explaining why Cursor's Stop-hook continuation cannot port to Claude Code; (2) rewrite skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py to a precondition-only shape -- run the authority chain, print an advisory to stdout/stderr describing next_unit or the reason to stop, always exit 0; (3) update skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md and skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md with a 'Claude Code: run under /loop' section, pointing Claude Code users at '/loop /invoke-development-harness'; (4) update skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 5 (Hook configuration) so the Claude Code install path wires the hook as a precondition checker and the generated .claude/settings.local.json reflects the new role; (5) PHASE_008 (Stop-hook fleet-awareness, units 034-036) currently assumes the Claude Code hook keeps driving continuation -- those units need to be re-scoped once this fix lands so their fleet-mode checks layer onto the precondition-checker shape, not the retired block-continue shape. Cursor installs are untouched: skills/development-harness/templates/hooks/continue-loop.py remains the canonical driver for Cursor; the followup_message path stays.
raw_text: ISSUE -- Claude Code Stop-hook continuation is a one-shot guard; harness auto-continue design is a direct Cursor port and is fundamentally incompatible with Claude Code's Stop hook protocol. Validated in-session: /invoke-development-harness auto-continues exactly once then stops, forcing the user to manually re-invoke every 1-2 units. Claude Code's stop_hook_active is a persistent one-shot flag and Claude Code's own loop-guard fires even if the hook tries to force-continue through it; there is no official opt-out. The existing .claude/hooks/continue-loop.py is a direct port of the Cursor variant whose followup_message protocol has no such guard. Fix: retire Stop-hook-as-driver for Claude Code, use the /loop skill as the autonomous-run primitive, keep the hook's authority chain as a precondition checker only. Add a references/claude-code-continuation.md doc, rewrite the Claude Code hook template, update commands/invoke.md and the workspace-commands template to point Claude Code users at '/loop /invoke-development-harness', update commands/create.md Phase 5 to install the hook as a precondition checker only. Cursor installs are untouched. Priority: high -- current roadmap assumes auto-continue is reliable; user is manually re-invoking every turn, materially slowing the dogfood loop.
